ShortDescription.Value: Rebuilds household affordability around transfer-preserving, attachment-gated expected earnings plus bounded durable-goods and liquidity signals.

Publish.LongDescription:
Permanent Income Hypothesis reworks household affordability in Cities: Skylines II around a broader concept of effective household means.
Instead of relying too heavily on current labor-like income and short-run cash, the mod is being built around:
- a transfer-preserving structural anchor for households with children or elderly residents - an attachment-gated expected-earnings anchor based on employment status, education, and lifecycle - a smoothed observed-income baseline to avoid overreacting to short-run shocks - weak durable-goods and bounded liquid-wealth signals - post-estimation housing-consistency diagnostics rather than housing-driven income inference - a household-level cache so multiple affordability systems can share one slower-moving result
The design goal is to reduce false NoMoney pressure and high-rent misclassification for affluent households in expensive housing without turning the game into a free-money faucet.

ChangeLog.Value: Initial repository scaffold with calculator, settings, docs, and generalized GitHub workflow structure.
